candidates.evidence_quote: Our revenue growth is primarily driven by new restaurant openings and increases in comparable restaurant sales.
primarily due to additional revenue related to new restaurant openings and an increase in comparable restaurant sales
North Italia comparable sales decreased approximately 3% from the third quarter of fiscal 2024.
Flower Child comparable sales increased approximately 7% from the third quarter of fiscal 2024.
partially offset by decreased customer traffic of 2.5%
primarily driven by an increase in average check of 2.8%
We implemented menu price increases of approximately 2.4% and 1.5% in the first and third quarters of fiscal 2025, respectively.
partially offset by a 1.7% negative change from menu mix
Sales through the off-premise channel comprised approximately 21% of our restaurant sales during both the third quarter of fiscal 2025 and fiscal 2024.
Total operating weeks at The Cheesecake Factory Restaurants increased 0.1% to 2,808 in the third quarter of fiscal 2025 compared to 2,804 in the prior year.
primarily due to favorable commodity inflation across most categories (0.5%)
This decrease was primarily due to menu price increases in excess of wage rate inflation and improved staffing levels and productivity (0.3%).
improved staffing levels and productivity
menu price increases in excess of wage rate inflation and improved staffing levels and productivity (0.6%)
partially offset by higher group medical costs due to larger claim activity (0.3%)
primarily driven by higher facility - related costs (0.4%)
Preopening costs can fluctuate significantly from period to period based on the number, mix and timing of restaurant openings
we recorded impairment of assets and lease termination expenses of $0.5 million primarily related to lease termination costs for one The Cheesecake Factory and one Other FRC location
Our effective income tax rate was 10.1% and 5.8% for the third quarter of fiscal 2025 and 2024, respectively.
These factors were offset by higher employment credits in the third quarter of fiscal 2025 (3.0%).
a change to our reserve for uncertain tax positions (2.7%)
We recorded a $15.9 million loss on early debt extinguishment in the first quarter of fiscal 2025.

returning capital to shareholders through our dividend and share repurchase programs, the latter of which offsets dilution from our equity compensation program and supports our earnings per share growth
Future decisions to pay or to increase or decrease dividends or to repurchase shares are at the discretion of the Board and will be dependent on a number of factors

Cash flows from operations increased by $52.1 million from the first nine months of fiscal 2024 primarily due to net income after excluding the non-cash activity, higher accrued expenses, timing of operating lease commencements, inventory movement and income tax payments
Capital expenditures for new restaurants, including locations under development, were $62.7 million and $76.1 million for the first nine months of fiscal 2025 and 2024, respectively.
increases in minimum wages and benefit costs

foreign exchange rates, tariffs and cross border taxation
changes in interest rates, periods of heightened inflation and market instability
economic, public health and political conditions that impact consumer confidence and spending, including government shutdowns

notes: Chunk contains a single event: 10-Q MD&A (accession 0001104659-25-105631), part 1 of 2; part 2 is handled by another bot and re-unioned later.
No fiscal_kpis in this chunk (KPIs appear only on chunk_001).

Skipped the namesake brand-level comp metric (The Cheesecake Factory comparable sales) because the brand name equals the company legal name, which is banned inside driver_name; company-wide same_store_sales covers it.

Some candidates share one evidence sentence that names multiple independent causal variables (per R2 split rule): tariff_policy/foreign_exchange_rate; consumer_confidence/consumer_spending; wage_inflation/labor_productivity/restaurant_staffing_levels.

Skipped (~15): brand total sales lines for North Italia/Flower Child/Other FRC (derivative of unit growth plus comps), stock_based_compensation and acquisition contingent consideration (table-only, no narrative attribution), pandemic/in-person dining restrictions, armed conflicts and political unrest (boilerplate), gift card program, Cheesecake Rewards loyalty program, third-party delivery partnership (initiatives without measured state), royalty revenue and bakery operations (no attributed change), landlord/tenant economic health (vague), average sales per restaurant operating week (derivative), G&A flat year-over-year, interest expense (minor line item), shifts in consumer behavior (vague).
